name: marimba-bar-a3
description: Tuned rosewood marimba bar, A3. The undercut arch of a tuned bar pulls the first three transverse modes to the published ratios 1 : 3.9 : 9.2 (an untuned rectangular bar sits near 1 : 2.76 : 5.40), which is what makes the bar read as a pitch rather than a clank. Modes are 220 Hz * {1, 3.9, 9.2}. No shape maps: strike and pickup position have no effect here, so every mode's authored gain is the amplitude a measurement of the impulse response reads back.
